fix: auto-update now detects prerelease versions (alpha/beta/rc)

Three root causes fixed:
- Changed from /releases/latest to /releases API — the /latest endpoint
  silently skips prerelease-tagged releases, so beta/rc versions were
  never found
- Added proper pre-release version comparison (alpha < beta < rc < release)
  so 1.0.0 is correctly detected as newer than 1.0.0-beta
- Release workflow now auto-detects prerelease tags (-alpha/-beta/-rc)

android: fix USB permission — request dialog before connecting

UsbSerial.java:
- Add requestPermissionAsync(): registers a BroadcastReceiver, calls
  UsbManager.requestPermission() showing the system Allow dialog, stores
  result in sPermissionGranted. Uses RECEIVER_NOT_EXPORTED on API 33+.
- Add hasPermission(ctx): checks UsbManager directly.
- Add isPermissionGranted(): C++ polls this flag.

BackendFactory (main.cpp):

  as Q_INVOKABLEs calling the Java statics above.

- Connect button disabled until hasUsbPermission() == true.

commit.message:
doc: add gateway design specification (#14)

* doc: add gateway design specification

New file docs/gateway-design.md covering:

- Technology choices: Rust, tokio, prevail-rust, ciborium, RustCrypto
- Module architecture: transport, protocol codec, session manager,
  node registry, program library, handler router, storage
- Abstract transport trait with ESP-NOW as first adapter
- Protocol codec: frame parsing, HMAC verification, CBOR message types
- Session manager: per-node sessions, sequence tracking, command
  selection, inbound frame processing flow
- Node registry: key lookup, node metadata, USB pairing support
- Program library: ingestion, prevail-rust verification, chunk serving
- Handler router: program_hash routing, process lifecycle, DATA/
  DATA_REPLY/EVENT/LOG message flow
- Abstract storage trait for persistence
- Concurrency model: single tokio runtime with per-node tasks
- Error handling: silent discard with leveled logging
- Configuration, startup, and shutdown sequences

All design decisions are traceable to gateway-requirements.md and
protocol.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* doc: fix transport trait to carry sender address

ESP-NOW uses MAC addresses for peer addressing. The transport trait
now returns the sender's address with each received frame, and send()
takes an explicit peer address. The session stores the peer address
so responses are sent to the correct MAC. This avoids a broken
key_hint-to-MAC mapping (key_hint can collide).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* doc: clarify MAC address is session-scoped, never persisted

The peer address (MAC) is stored in the session only for the duration
of the wake cycle. key_hint + HMAC is the durable identity mechanism;
MAC is a transient delivery address that is re-established on each
WAKE.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* doc: update gateway design for CBOR program image format

- ProgramRecord.bytes renamed to .image, clarified as CBOR-encoded
- Ingestion pipeline: add ELF extraction, LDDW relocation resolution,
  CBOR encoding, and deterministic encoding steps
- Size limits apply to the CBOR image, not the ELF
- Hash computed over CBOR image
- Link to protocol.md program image format section
- Chunk serving reads from stored CBOR image

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* doc: add key store encryption at rest requirement

- Add GW-0601a: key store SHOULD encrypt PSKs at rest (from
  security.md 2.3, was not captured as a gateway requirement)
- Update gateway-design.md Storage section: implementations are
  responsible for encrypting NodeRecord.psk before persisting

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* doc: address PR #14 review feedback

- HandlerConfig: replace Vec<Vec<u8>> with ProgramMatcher enum
  (Any | Hash) to properly model catch-all routing
- Session map: specify tokio::sync::RwLock (not std), note MUST NOT

- GW-0601a: drop incorrect GW-1001 cross-reference for operator

commit.message:
Multi-selection v1: Ctrl+click additive select, group operations

State model: STATE.selectedId (number|null)  STATE.selectedIds (Set)
with primarySelectedId() helper for workbench context.

Selection: Ctrl/Cmd+click toggles elements in/out of selection.
Plain click replaces selection. Ctrl+A selects all.

Group operations: delete, flip H/V, duplicate, lock/unlock, and
layer reorder all operate on the full selection set. Lock toggles
use any-unlocked heuristic (if any unlocked  lock all, else unlock).
Duplicate clones all selected with offset, selects all clones.
Arrow nudge moves all selected elements together.

Overlay: single selection shows full handles (resize/rotate/edge).
Multi-selection shows per-element dashed bounding boxes without
handles  move only, no resize/rotate.

Workbenches: carve and ink panels show 'N elements selected' message
when multi-selected. Single selection retains full editing. Carve
focus requires single selection.

Group drag: all selected unlocked elements move together with the
existing 6px dead zone. Undo captures all element positions.

commit.message:
fix: use sync psycopg2 driver for alembic migrations

Root cause: asyncpg driver records alembic_version but DDL operations
silently fail to commit, leaving the database without tables while
alembic thinks migrations have been applied.

Fix: Switch alembic env.py from async (asyncpg) to sync (psycopg2)
connection for running migrations. This is the standard recommendation
for alembic with async SQLAlchemy apps.

Changes:
- alembic/env.py: use create_engine with psycopg2 instead of
  async_engine_from_config with asyncpg
- requirements.txt: add psycopg2-binary==2.9.10
- app/main.py: add startup table verification that logs CRITICAL
  error if required tables are missing after migration

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: require delete confirmation before removing a product (#3)

Deleting a product was immediate with no way to recover — a single
misclick would permanently remove an entry.

## Changes

- **New `showDeleteConfirmModal()`** — wires up a modal with
confirm/cancel/backdrop-dismiss, cleans up all listeners after each
interaction to prevent accumulation on repeated opens
- **Delete handler** now calls `showDeleteConfirmModal()` instead of
invoking `deleteProduct()` directly
- **Modal HTML** appended to the `initApp` template, styled with
existing Tailwind utilities (fixed overlay + centered card)

```ts
// Before
deleteProduct(id);
renderProductList(container);

// After
showDeleteConfirmModal(id, container);
// → modal confirm → deleteProduct(id) + renderProductList(container)
// → modal cancel / backdrop click → dismiss, no-op
```

add a hook system into FSM transition events for things like... (#42)

* feat(hooks): add TransitionEvent, HookRunner interface, HookRegistry, and wire into FSM

* feat(task-2): add WebhookHook implementation (HTTP POST with JSON payload)

partial: tests blocked on sibling notify_hook.go import cycle (session->config/taskfsm)

* feat(hooks): add NotifyHook (desktop notifications) and CommandHook (shell commands)

NotifyHook: platform-aware desktop notification on each FSM transition via
a replaceable notifyFunc. Avoids a session→cmd→config/taskfsm import cycle by
inlining the minimal OS notification dispatch (mirrors session.SendNotification)
and exposing a DefaultNotifyFunc package var for external wiring.

CommandHook: executes an arbitrary shell command through 'sh -c' on each
transition, injecting KASMOS_HOOK_{PLAN,EVENT,FROM,TO,PROJECT} env vars.
Empty commands are rejected eagerly; context cancellation and timeouts
surface naturally from exec.CommandContext.

* feat(task-4): add TOML/JSON config, hook factory, and wire into signal processor

* fix: resolve TestWebhookHook_RespectsContextTimeout deadlock (reviewer self-fix)

Go's HTTP server does not cancel r.Context() in a non-reading handler when
the client closes the connection. Replace the bare <-r.Context().Done() with
a select that also listens on a cleanup channel, and close that channel in
t.Cleanup before srv.Close() — preventing the WaitGroup drain from blocking
indefinitely.

* Add best practices for React and server-side development

- Introduced guidelines for calculating derived state during rendering to avoid redundant renders.
- Added recommendations for subscribing to derived state to reduce re-render frequency.
- Emphasized the use of functional setState updates to prevent stale closures and unnecessary callback recreations.
- Suggested lazy state initialization for expensive initial values to optimize performance.
- Provided guidance on extracting default non-primitive parameter values from memoized components to restore memoization.
- Encouraged extracting expensive work into memoized components to enable early returns.
- Recommended placing interaction logic in event handlers to avoid unnecessary effect re-runs.
- Advised against wrapping simple expressions in useMemo to prevent wasted computation.
- Promoted the use of transitions for non-urgent updates to maintain UI responsiveness.
- Suggested using useRef for transient values to avoid unnecessary re-renders.
- Introduced the use of after() for non-blocking operations in server actions.
- Highlighted the importance of authenticating server actions like API routes to prevent unauthorized access.
- Implemented cross-request LRU caching for shared data across sequential requests.
- Recommended per-request deduplication with React.cache() for efficient data fetching.
- Advised avoiding duplicate serialization in RSC props to reduce network payload.
- Encouraged parallel data fetching with component composition to eliminate server-side waterfalls.
- Suggested minimizing serialization at RSC boundaries to reduce data transfer size.
- Added a new skill for reviewing UI code against Web Interface Guidelines compliance.

* Add best practices for React and server-side development

- Introduced guidelines for calculating derived state during rendering to avoid redundant renders.
- Added recommendations for subscribing to derived state to reduce re-render frequency.
- Emphasized the use of functional setState updates to prevent stale closures and unnecessary callback recreations.
- Suggested lazy state initialization for expensive initial values to optimize performance.
- Provided guidance on extracting default non-primitive parameter values from memoized components to restore memoization.
- Encouraged extracting expensive work into memoized components to enable early returns.
- Recommended placing interaction logic in event handlers to avoid unnecessary effect re-runs.
- Advised against wrapping simple expressions in useMemo to prevent wasted computation.
- Promoted the use of transitions for non-urgent updates to maintain UI responsiveness.
- Suggested using useRef for transient values to avoid unnecessary re-renders.
- Introduced the use of after() for non-blocking operations in server actions.
- Highlighted the importance of authenticating server actions like API routes to prevent unauthorized access.
- Implemented cross-request LRU caching for shared data across sequential requests.
- Recommended per-request deduplication with React.cache() for efficient data fetching.
- Advised avoiding duplicate serialization in RSC props to reduce network payload.
- Encouraged parallel data fetching with component composition to eliminate server-side waterfalls.
- Suggested minimizing serialization at RSC boundaries to reduce data transfer size.
- Added a new skill for reviewing UI code against Web Interface Guidelines compliance.

commit.message:
Fix parallel session crash: coalesce state notifications + dispose Process handles (#337)

## Problem

When running many CLI sessions in parallel (5+), the app progressively
freezes and crashes.

### Root causes

1. **Render flood** — Each session fires OnStateChanged on every SDK
event. With N parallel sessions, the WebView WebContent process gets
starved by rapid Blazor re-renders (241 updateFreezerStatus errors in
30min).

2. **Process handle leaks** — ServerManager never disposed Process
objects. OS handles accumulated across reconnect cycles (468 unobserved
exceptions in crash log).

## Fix

### Coalesced state notifications
Added NotifyStateChangedCoalesced() — a 150ms timer-based coalescer for
high-frequency, non-critical state updates. Applied to:
- ToolExecutionStartEvent phase change (Working)
- AssistantTurnStartEvent phase change (Thinking)
- ToolExecutionCompleteEvent permission denial notification
- WsBridgeClient.OnStateChanged remote session sync

Critical events remain immediate: session completion, errors, session
switches, watchdog.

### Process handle disposal
- ServerManager.StartServerAsync: dispose Process after stdout/stderr
streams close
- ServerManager.StopServer: dispose after stopping
- DevTunnelService.Stop/TryHostTunnelAsync: dispose before nulling

## Testing
Build: 0 errors. Tests: 2378 passed (4 pre-existing failures).

Fixes #336

fix: clean up stale server.pid in relaunch.ps1 (#343)

## Problem

When the persistent copilot server dies unexpectedly, the \server.pid\
file can become stale — referencing a dead process. This causes
PolyPilot to fail to connect on relaunch because it thinks a server
exists but port 4321 isn't actually listening.

## Solution

Now \ elaunch.ps1\ checks if the server is actually listening before
build. If \server.pid\ exists but the port isn't responding, the stale
file is removed so PolyPilot will auto-start a fresh server on launch.

## Testing

1. Kill the copilot server manually (without deleting server.pid)
2. Run \elaunch.ps1\
3. Verify it detects the stale PID and cleans up
4. Verify PolyPilot starts a fresh server and connects successfully
